content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Charles A. Szychowski 
4215 Mobley Avenue 
La Sierra, CA 92505-1749 
Dear Mr. Szychowski: 
December 22, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. G-89-684 
This is in response to your request for information 
concerning American Independent Party candidates for statewide 
offices. 
Candidates must file a candidate intention statement (Form 
501) with the Fair Political Practices commission prior to 
soliciting or receiving any contributions. 
In addition, they are 
required to open campaign bank accounts and file campaign bank 
account statements (Form 502). 
(Gov. Code section 85201.) 
However, the law does not require candidates to list party 
affiliation on these forms. 
Enclosed is a copy the printout which 
shows the names of all candidates for statewide office. 
If you have any questions regarding this matter, please call 
me at (916) 322-5662. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Charles A. Szychowski 
4215 Mobley Avenue 
December 4, 1989 
La Sierra, CA 
92505-1749 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-684 
Dear Mr. Szychowski: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on November 27, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about yqur advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more in'formation is needed, you should expect a response wi thin 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
J'€anne Pr i tchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

sections.conclusion: Candidates must file a candidate intention statement (Form 501) with the Fair Political Practices Commission prior to soliciting or receiving any contributions. In addition, they are required to open campaign bank accounts and file campaign bank account statements (Form 502). (Gov. Code section 85201.) However, the law does not require candidates to list party affiliation on these forms.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked whether there are any American Independent Party candidates who have filed candidate intention statements for statewide offices in the 1990 election, and whether the FPPC can provide information about their campaign bank accounts.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirmed that candidates must file Form 501 and Form 502 documents, but the law does not require party affiliation to be listed on these forms. A printout of all statewide candidates was enclosed.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple pages including an initial acknowledgment letter (December 4, 1989) and the substantive response (December 22, 1989). The question is not presented in a labeled QUESTION section but is inferred from the response content and request letter fragments visible in the document. Some document text is partially illegible/faint, affecting full extraction confidence.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked whether there are any American Independent Party candidates who have filed candidate intention statements for statewide offices in the 1990 election, and whether the FPPC can provide information about their campaign bank accounts.

CONCLUSION: Candidates must file a candidate intention statement (Form 501) with the Fair Political Practices Commission prior to soliciting or receiving any contributions. In addition, they are required to open campaign bank accounts and file campaign bank account statements (Form 502). (Gov. Code section 85201.) However, the law does not require candidates to list party affiliation on these forms.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Charles Szychowski's inquiry about American Independent Party candidates for statewide office by explaining candidate filing requirements (Form 501 and 502) and clarifying that party affiliation is not required to be listed on these forms, while providing an enclosed printout of all statewide candidates.
